task_goal: Map open relation proposals to candidate schema relation types, or drop them when no clear mapping exists and the relation is not important enough to keep.
global_constraints: You may only choose relation_type values from the candidate_relations of that proposal.
Use only the input text and proposal evidence; do not invent facts.
If the correct schema direction reverses the current subject/object order, set swap_subject_object=true.
If there is no clear mapping, or the relation is too minor / low-value, choose drop.
grounding_rules: Prefer the schema relation type that most directly matches the textual evidence.
Keep relation_name as a short natural-language phrase; you may reuse the original relation phrase or lightly normalize it.
Use description as a one-sentence justification grounded in the text.
If the open relation is just a local interaction detail while the schema mainly captures higher-level event / participation / location relations, you may drop it.

examples.description: The character directly carries out the event described in the text.
The text supports that the event or occasion happens at that location, so the endpoints should be reversed.
